description_long: This is a tall, bulbous ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a single, curved handle attached from the neck to the shoulder. The surface is decorated with hand-painted motifs in green, yellow, and black, including stylized leaves, feathers, and a bird, as well as calligraphic text. The rim is slightly flared, and the base is rounded, tapering gently to a small foot. The handle features alternating green and yellow stripes, adding a vibrant accent to the overall design. The glaze appears matte, with visible brushstrokes and some minor surface imperfections, suggesting artisanal craftsmanship. The vase has a rustic, traditional appearance, likely intended as a decorative piece for display.
description: A hand-painted, bulbous ceramic vase with a single striped handle, decorated with stylized leaves, a bird, and calligraphic text in green, yellow, and black. The surface is matte and artisanal, with a rustic, traditional look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the calligraphic text '1904' and a large green and yellow leaf motif, with the handle partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the striped handle and more of the calligraphic text, with the leaf motif wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: Side view focusing on the painted bird and surrounding foliage, with the handle receding to the left.
description_view_3: Rear view dominated by the painted bird and leafy motifs, with the handle mostly hidden from sight.
attribute_pattern: floral scrollwork with bird and calligraphy
